bullets.text: Q1 CR 94.1 — acceptable result despite rising severity trends; personal auto running ~0.5 pts above pricing expectations; unfavorable reserve development partially offset by prior year favorable development; 96 target maintained.

Written premium grew 7% in Q1; Agency new business up 8% with stronger conversion; Direct new business up ~4%; Commercial Auto double-digit growth (+13% written premium) with CR 92.4 — economy reclaiming lost ground.

Severity trends increasing across most coverages; frequency still generally favorable but acknowledged as a 'dangerous combination that can change quickly'; catastrophe tornadoes already hitting early in 2012 given unusual weather patterns.
Comprehensive income up 17% YOY; investment total return 3.3% — considerably ahead of prior year; $1.3B+ in unrealized portfolio gains; $350M debt tranche retired in early 2012 reducing leverage.

Snapshot device shipments setting new quarterly records; mobile quoting advancing including camera-capture prototype for driver's license and insurance card for near-instant quotes; more than 15% of Progressive search activity mobile-based.

Agency renewal growth solid; Direct renewals very strong; new business applications roughly flat in March suggesting advertising environment highly competitive with 'significant dollars chasing new business.'

Continuous rate review by state and distribution channel; several largest states running slightly above target — appropriate rate actions already filed; philosophy of moving ahead of competitors on rate maintained.

Snapshot national rollout building agency acceptance; Name Your Price providing differentiated quoting experience; Progressive Home Advantage multi-product expansion continuing to drive household measures to new highs.
Industry full-year CR estimated north of 100 with ~1.5% premium growth; Progressive CR 94.1 against market-wide unprofitability positions company for opportunistic growth in hardening market.
Claims organization absorbing tornado catastrophes early in 2012 while maintaining quality scores; NPS scores moving in right direction; operationally well-staffed across most functions.
